sanskrit:
भवाप्ययौ हि भूतानां श्रुतौ विस्तरशो मया।
त्वत्तः कमलपत्राक्ष माहात्म्यमपि चाव्ययम्॥
transliteration:
bhavāpyayau hi bhūtānāṃ śrutau vistaraśo mayā |
tvattaḥ kamala-patrākṣa māhātmyam api cāvyayam ||
literal_meaning: O lotus-eyed one, I have heard from You in detail the appearance and disappearance of beings, and also Your inexhaustible greatness.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna expresses that he has cognitively integrated Krishna’s teaching on the eternal cycle of creation and dissolution, and now feels secure enough to request a direct vision of that cosmic process.
psychological_state: A stabilized post-insight state—cognitive assimilation of impermanence paired with devotional trust, allowing curiosity to override residual dread.
trigger_scenario: After therapy sessions on mortality anxiety, a client suddenly feels safe enough to ask, 'Show me what death really looks like,' trusting the therapist to hold the frame.
anchor_text: Trust the eternal rhythm of creation and dissolution.
